title: Framework capability contract (Phase-4 native-E2E, design §2/§13)
description: Machine-readable, framework-OWNED declaration of the capabilities the DEPLOYED aidazi provides. Preflight (--sign-plan) and the real-run gate check charter.required_framework_capabilities against this contract DETERMINISTICALLY + FAIL-CLOSED (a missing/under-versioned/malformed/unreadable contract REFUSES). Capability IDENTITY is anchored to CODE (each capability names a code_anchor a build-time test resolves), so it does NOT depend only on mutable documentation text.

$schema_ref.description: Informational pointer to this schema (relative repo path).

contract_version.description: Schema/contract revision of THIS file's shape.

framework_version.description: The deployed aidazi framework version, surfaced in a capability refusal so the adopter knows what they pinned.

capabilities.description: The capabilities this framework build PROVIDES. An adopter references these ids in charter.required_framework_capabilities.

version.description: Capability version (dotted integers, compared component-wise vs an adopter's min_version).

code_anchor.description: <repo-relative-path>:<symbol> — the def/class that PROVES the capability is wired in code (identity is code-anchored, not doc text).

summary.description: One-line human summary (advisory; NOT the identity — the code_anchor is).
